Petition To Waive Appraisal Of Decedent, Rose Paks Interest In Thee Estate Of Robert Ralph Saucci
Off Calendar Feb-24-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Long appeared by zoom for Teresa Wong (Petitioner).
Amy Harrington (Probate Referee) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Konstantine Demiris appeared by zoom for John Balquist (Petitioner/Administrator).

The Court asked if anyone is objecting to waiving of the appraisal. Ms. Harrington stated that in general Probate Referee's object to waiving appraisals of any matter and noted that the funds that were received are no evidence of the value of the Decedent's interest what so ever. Mr. Long stated that has no objection. The Petition to Waive Appraisal of Decedent's Interest in the Estate of Robert Ralph Saucci is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-27-2020 Continued To Aug-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris appeared by zoom for John Balquist (Administrator).

The Court informed Mr. Demiris that he needs to file either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration and that even though he filed a report, it needs to be in the form of a petition. Mr. Demiris requested a 90-day continuance stating that the only hold up is the probate in Hong Kong, that Amy Harrington (Probate Referee) has requested a copy of the will and that Hong Kong keeps wills private. Mr. Demiris also stated that they are working with the Hong Kong Attorney, but they have been unable to get a copy of the will because the Attorney refuses to provide one, that they are working with the Beneficiaries in trying to work things out amongst themselves and that the parties are willing to waive the appraisal of the Referee. The Court informed Mr. Demiris that there is a procedure in the Probate Code for waiving appraisals. Status on close of estate is continued to August 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Continued To Nov-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Heather Maslowski appearing by CourtCall for John Balquist.

The Court stated that on calendar before it today is status on the filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition to approve further report on status of administration. Ms. Maslowski stated that there were two issues holding up the status on close of the estate, that they were not able to sell the property at 400 Beale Street, that the property was a low income property, that the property was finally sold on 6/19/19, that there is an international probate in Hong Kong pending, that it involves the Father's estate, that it is $50,000.00, that she has spoken with the Hong Kong attorneys, and that things should wrap up in four to six weeks. The Court continued today's status to November 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the filing of either a petition for final distribution or a petition to approve further report on status of administration.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-04-2019 Continued To Mar-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Heather Maslowski for John Balquist (Administrator).
Christina Chen for and with Joanna Pak Kish (Objector).

The Court stated that counsel filed an amended notice, that there was non-compliance with Probate Code 12201(b), and that it was mentioned at the last hearing. Ms. Maslowski stated that she had spoken with Ms. Chen. Ms. Chen stated that they waive objections to notice, that it's form over matter, that there are no other waivers from her clients, that she'd spoken with Brian Call (counsel for Teresa Wang), and that Mr. Call agrees without objection that the administration is taking too long. The Court stated that it needed courtesy copies of the objections, that it would continue the matter for notice, and that it had to continue the matter as there is mandatory language missing. Ms. Maslowski stated that the real estate property asset is wrapped up with the the City of San Francisco's Housing Department, that the 400 Beale - Apt. 1 property was in the lottery process twice, that the lottery was won twice, that there were issues, that the first time there were qualifications that weren't met, and that the second time there were issues because the buyer requested $70,000.00 off the price. Ms. Chen replied stating that the administrator was appointed two years ago, that it took her one year to give Joanna the key so that Joanna could clean the property (etc.), that the City lotteries are held every two to three months, and with what's happened that everything has been well within the Administrator's control. The Court ordered an updated status report from Ms. Maslowski and ordered Ms. Maslowski to comply with the Probate Code regarding the notice issues. The Court then continued the matter to March 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for notice/compliance with Probate Code 12201(b) and an updated status report from Ms. Maslowski.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Continued To Mar-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Heather Maslowski for John Balquist (Administrator).
Christina Chen for Joanna Pak Kish (Beneficiary).

Ms. Chen stated that she is here to express her Client's concerns, that she is not filing objections, that this is taking to long, that the Administrator was appointed in January of 2017 and that the condominium is only now being sold. Ms. Chen also stated that the problem is that the association fees are over $1,000.00 a month, that this is a small estate and that it is depleting the estate. Ms. Maslowski stated that she is asking for a 90-day extension to sell the property and 120-day extension to close the estate, explained that she is working with the Mayor's Office because this is a below market rate property, that she has to follow the lottery process and the property must be sold by lottery, and that she filed an amended status report last week. Ms. Chen stated that the lottery system goes every two to three months, that she is objecting to Ms. Maslowski's request for 180-days to close the estate and that she is requesting that it be closed in 90-days. The Court informed Ms. Maslowski that she has to give notice of the amended petition, that she has to serve the Attorneys and also the parties, and that the proof of service has to include the required language under the Probate Code. The Amended Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is continued to March 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION

Ms. Maslowski stated that she filed the proof of service last week and that she filed a status report, but she was not aware of any requirement to provide a proposed order. Ms. Maslowski also stated that her Client is working with the Mayor's Office of Housing and Community Development, that this is a below market property and that at this point they are on the second lottery winner. The Petition for Report on Status of Administration is continued to February 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for review of the proof of service and receipt of a proposed order. Ms. Maslowski is to provide a paper copy of the proposed order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
